name: Veracruz Salsa (Tomato Chile Salsa)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon coarse salt, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a dry comal or flat iron griddle over moderately low heat until hot.
2. Roast garlic and tomato, turning occasionally for even roasting, until browned and soft throughout, about 25 to 30 minutes. Remove from skins and discard the tomato stem.
3. In a heavy skillet, heat oil over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Fry chiles (1 or 2 at a time), turning them, until puffed and just beginning to brown, about 10 seconds per side. Drain on paper towels and discard stems, wearing rubber gloves.
4. Transfer the roasted garlic, tomato, fried chiles, water, and salt to a blender. Blend until very smooth.
5. Salsa can be made ahead of time, up to 2 days, covered in the refrigerator.
6. Serve chilled. Makes about 2 cups.
